subtitle: Evidence-based genomic interval correspondence
libraryDesc: FASTA / GFF3 references available on this PC
selectRoot: Detect genomes on this PC or enter a custom folder.
setupDesc: Define the source interval and independent evidence.
settingsDesc: Runtime paths and external analysis capabilities.
monitorDesc: Queued, active, and completed local analysis jobs.
spacingHelp: Anchors are genes kept at least the minimum spacing apart (no upper cap — a wider interval yields more). If a narrow or gene-sparse interval falls below the minimum anchor count, the spacing tightens to guarantee that many genes, spread evenly.
allGenesHelp: Maps every annotated gene overlapping the selected source interval.
markerHint: DNA sequence only (FASTA or raw bases)
contigSearchHint: Type a name to filter candidates
stepIntervalHint: The interval on reference B to map. Coordinates are 1-based, inclusive. Peak is optional.
presetHintShort: See the How-it-works tab for per-preset anchor placement. Without markers, confidence is capped at Medium.
liftoverHint: Adds an independent third line of evidence. The first run for a reference pair builds a whole-genome alignment (slow); later runs reuse the cache.
liftoverUnavailable: minimap2 is unavailable, so liftover cannot be used.
coordRequired: Enter both start and end coordinates.
coordOrder: End must be greater than or equal to start.
peakRange: Peak must fall inside the interval.
providerAuto: Automatically selects an available local BLAST runtime.
providerWindows: Uses Windows blastn against target A.
providerWsl: Uses blastn inside WSL against target A.
providerExact: For artificial data and exact-match validation.
providerNotice: Available providers are local Windows BLAST+, WSL BLAST+, and exact fallback. NCBI remote BLAST is not implemented.
evidenceSummaryCaption: Each lane is an independent line of evidence (liftover / markers / synteny). The x-axis is the target-A coordinate, the shaded band is the final inferred interval, and the number on the right is each line's support count.
anchorPlotCaption: Each dot is a uniquely-mapped anchor gene. A lower-left to upper-right trend means forward orientation; a downward trend indicates an inversion.
intervalPlotCaption: Top = source-B interval, bottom = target-A interval. Lines link each anchor's correspondence; colour shows orientation (green = forward, orange = reverse).
confidenceTip: Confidence reflects how well the independent lines of evidence (synteny, markers, liftover) agree. See the How-it-works tab for details.
oriCountsTip: Number of anchors matching in the forward (→) and reverse (←) direction.
